word: G2361 - θρόμβος - thrómbos - throm'-bos - Great drop

sections.paragraph:
- Original: θρόμβος

- Transliteration: Thrombos

- Phonetic: throm'-bos

- Definition:

1. a large thick drop, esp. of clotted blood

- Origin: perhaps from G5142 (in the sense of thickening)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Perhaps from G5142 (in the sense of thickening); a clot: - great drop.
